recovered_description.label: VdM 19600005
recovered_description.gt: Black-figure body sherd from a large, closed form, probably an amphora. The decoration has the right edge of the frame of the main field of decoration. There is a small fragment of a draped figure to the left, with the folds of the drapery picked out in incisions. Running next to and behind the figure is a rectangular object that is impossible to pick out. Possibly from the same amphora as VdM 19600002 and VdM 19600003. On its own, the sherd is impossible to date/attribute.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment of a black gloss pottery vessel with a triangular shape. The exterior surface is partially covered with black slip, which is worn in some areas. The fragment is flat with a slight tapering edge, similar to other Greek gloss body fragments from the Murlo project.

recovered_description.label: Object 1987_4:12
recovered_description.project: 3D History for Ukraine: Archaeological Museum of the Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv
recovered_description.gt: Boat-shaped stone axe with the emphasized rounded poll and a thick wedge-shaped blade. The planned location of the hole placed closer to the poll.
recovered_description.pred: Stone object, possibly an axe or hammer, with a roughly triangular shape and a flat base. It features a narrow edge and a central hole, similar to other stone tools from the same project.

recovered_description.label: PC 20040056
recovered_description.gt: A rocchetto preserves slightly ovoid heads that come to rounded edges before tapering inward to the shaft.
recovered_description.pred: An intact rocchetto with two slightly flared heads, both showing significant chips and wear. The surface is worn, pitted, and encrusted with some inclusions.

recovered_description.label: PC 20030015
recovered_description.gt: An intact spindle whorl preserves a slightly convex truncated conical shape and is pierced with a cylindrical hole through the center. The top displays a circular depression around the hole 0.003 m in width, as does the base, though 0.005 m in width. Bordering the circular depression on the base is a convex band approximately 0.007 m in width divided into four finished inverted trianguar areas alternating with four triangular areas composed of roughly 10 slanted incised lines coming to points at the edge of the base.
recovered_description.pred: A spindle whorl with a circular shape and central hole. The surface displays a pattern of incised lines forming a chevron design around its circumference. The object is intact with worn and slightly pitted surfaces, and some encrustation is present.

recovered_description.label: SF t104-2023-2-7
recovered_description.gt: Pan Tile Fragment with Siglum
recovered_description.pred: Pantile fragment with siglum

recovered_description.label: Feat. 33989
recovered_description.gt: Big rectangular lime stone threshold ,in the westren north side of silo building complex , in wall
recovered_description.pred: Rectangular limestone feature with compact surface, possibly a niche or foundation, similar to other features with crushed limestone fill.

recovered_description.label: Sherd D-7-35-2-2
recovered_description.pred: ribbed shoulder with RB was decoration

recovered_description.label: PC 19840032
recovered_description.gt: Rocchetto fragment preserves a domed head and part of the shaft. There is a stamped design covering almost the entire head. At the center is a small stamp of two concentric circles, Around the center circle there are eight stamped triangles, apex facing outward; each is independently stamped forming a burst pattern. There is a small hole drilled through the shaft just above the break off point.
recovered_description.pred: Rocchetto fragment with a domed head that comes to a rounded edge before tapering into the shaft. The surface is worn and chipped, with a hole near the edge of the head.

recovered_description.label: PC 19820062
recovered_description.gt: Ridge pole tile fragment with incised design. The design consists of a circle tangential to which is a curved triangular form, concave nearest to the circle and on base, third side convex.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment of a ridge pole tile with incised curved lines. The surface shows traces of dark paint. The incisions are fragmentary, suggesting a decorative pattern similar to concentric circles or spirals.

recovered_description.label: DT# 5137
recovered_description.gt: Constantine I OBV: IMP CONSTANTINVS P F AVG, laureatte, draped, cuirassed, bust right REV: SOLI INV-I-CTO COMITI, Sol standing, chlamys across left shoulder, raising right hand, holding glode in left hand, S to left, F to right || Constantine I OBV: IMP CONSTANTINVS P F AVG, laureatte, draped, cuirassed, bust right REV: SOLI INV-I-CTO COMITI, Sol standing, chlamys across left shoulder, raising right hand, holding glode in left hand, R X to left, F to right
recovered_description.pred: Constantius II OBV: [FL IVL] CONSTANTIVS NOB C, laureate, cuirassed bust right REV: GLOR-IA EXERC-ITVS, two soldiers holding spears and shields on either side of two standards

recovered_description.label: PC 19840198
recovered_description.gt: Fragment of cut out akroterion preserves two curved finished edges - probably from the body of the volute.
recovered_description.pred: Akroterion fragment reconstructed from two pieces, rectangular in cross-section with rough, unfinished surfaces. The fragment is thick and flat, suggesting it may have been part of a larger architectural element.

recovered_description.label: PC 19860033
recovered_description.gt: A triangular ivory inlay. Both sides are flat.
recovered_description.pred: A triangular ivory inlay. Both surfaces are worn and chipped. The upper surface is slightly convex with visible wear patterns.

recovered_description.label: PC 20120116
recovered_description.gt: Worked bone fragment preserves roughly half of a disc with central, circular perforation 0.012 m in diameter. The worked edge is slightly beveled, and the narrower face of the disc preserves parallel, circular striations from turning while being worked.
recovered_description.pred: Half of a bone disc with a central perforation, restored from five fragments. The surfaces are worn and scratched, with visible cracks and slight encrustation. The mottled color suggests age and exposure. Similar to other spindle whorls from the Murlo project.

recovered_description.label: SF t102-2023-8-39
recovered_description.gt: Incised pottery sherd
recovered_description.pred: Incised ceramic sherd

recovered_description.label: PC 20070042
recovered_description.gt: A small, disc-shaped fragment of carbonzed wood is preserved with one flat surface and one irregular surface.
recovered_description.pred: Carbonized seed fragment, possibly grape, with a dark, friable surface and some encrustation.

recovered_description.label: PC 20180015
recovered_description.gt: A fine impasto rocchetto head fragment. The central portion of the head is flat with a bevel 0.006 m in length which turns into a convex curve where the fragment transitions from the head to the shaft. The shaft's max. pres. length is 0.021 m.
recovered_description.pred: A fragment of a rocchetto, likely made of impasto, with a worn and encrusted surface. The piece shows signs of ancient transverse break through the shaft and modern damage from excavation tools. The head is rounded and the shaft appears to have been tapered. The surface is scratched and chipped, indicating significant wear over time.

recovered_description.label: PC 20120215
recovered_description.gt: A very hard, shiny, irregularly grooved and ridged fragment, possibly runoff from the smelting of iron, is preserved. Surfaces are significantly denser and smoother than iron slag.
recovered_description.pred: Amorphous fragment of iron slag with irregular, pitted surfaces and small areas of corrosion and oxidation.

recovered_description.label: PC 19850071
recovered_description.gt: Pan tile preserves complete width and one finished end.
recovered_description.pred: Pan tile restored from numerous fragments, preserving partial width and portions of flanges. The tile is broken off at one end, with visible cracks from restoration. No decorative elements or markings are visible on the surface.

recovered_description.label: PC 19840026
recovered_description.gt: Statue fragment has two flat surfaces and curved rounded side edges. There is a design incised with wide grooves on one side. Two lines are parallel to and near each of the curved edges, and they border a zigzag pattern. The other surface is unfinished. Possibly a wing fragment.
recovered_description.pred: A fragment of an akroterion with a rough, worn surface. The piece is broken off on two ends and lacks any visible decoration. The fragment appears to taper slightly, suggesting it may have been part of a larger architectural element.

recovered_description.label: PC 20230146
recovered_description.gt: Terracotta cover tile fragment A preserves full width. It also preserves a convex exterior finished face and a concave interior finished face. Two longitudinal finished edges are preserved, as well as a portion on one lateral finished edge. Fragment B preserves a convex exterior finished face and a concave interior finished face, as well as a portion on one lateral finished edge. The exterior face may preserve paint. Fragment C preserves a convex exterior finished face and a concave interior finished face. On the exterior face appears to be a curving finger impression, approx. 0.00549m thick, which is possibly a spiral. Fragments D preserves one finished face. Fragments E and F each preserve an exterior finished face and an interior finished face. Fragments G - I are amorphous and provide no additional information.
recovered_description.pred: Terracotta cover tile composed of multiple fragments, preserving full length and width. The exterior face is convex and finished, while the interior face is concave and unfinished. Some fragments are associated but non-joining. The tile shows signs of ancient breaks and repairs.

recovered_description.label: PC 20110063
recovered_description.gt: Forno fragment preserves portion of the broad, flat rim that makes a rounded edge before continuing down the exterior of the fragment. The rim projects sharply over the interior surface which is slightly more irregular.
recovered_description.pred: Forno fragment with heavily worn and pitted surfaces, showing numerous inclusions throughout the fabric. The fragment is irregular in shape, with no clearly defined edges or surfaces preserved. The condition suggests significant exposure to heat and weathering, consistent with pyrotechnic use.

recovered_description.label: 94-P-39(226)
recovered_description.gt: Upper segment with deeply molded ridging to filling hole
recovered_description.pred: Fragment with deeply incised ridges, likely part of a larger ceramic object. Similar to shoulder fragments with ridged lines from Petra Great Temple Excavations.

recovered_description.label: PC 19720236
recovered_description.gt: Nearly complete banquet frieze plaque.
recovered_description.pred: Fragmentary frieze plaque composed of 13 fragments, depicting a banquet scene. Missing piece from left table and middle of right half; cavetto chipped. Similar to other Murlo project plaques, possibly featuring figures and furniture elements typical of banquet scenes.

recovered_description.label: Item 1280
recovered_description.project: The Amulets of the Kerma Culture
recovered_description.gt: The leg with the foot is pierced crosswise. No details are represented, including the toes and only the profile of the leg is evident. The surface of the amulet is smooth
recovered_description.pred: Necklace composed of carnelian beads of varying sizes and shapes, including spherical and irregular forms. The beads are strung together, likely pierced lengthwise, with some showing a polished surface. The necklace is part of the Kerma Culture amulets collection.

recovered_description.label: PC 20110078
recovered_description.gt: Curved fragment of a cut-out akroterion preserves a broader, flat, finished front surface and a narrower, more irregular back surface. The edges are slightly beveled.
recovered_description.pred: Akroterion fragment with a rough, irregular surface and visible inclusions throughout the fabric. The fragment is less finished on the back surface, which is more pitted and uneven.

recovered_description.label: VdM 19600198
recovered_description.gt: Fragmented iron fragment in a triangular shape. Appears to be a heavily corroded and broken sword. Context: Based on a description of the tomb’s contents by A. De Agostino (1960: 441) it is possible this comes from Tomb II in Tinoni.
recovered_description.pred: A fragment of iron blade, triangular in shape, with a pointed tip and irregular surface. The artifact shows signs of corrosion and wear, consistent with other iron tools or weapons from the Murlo project.

recovered_description.label: PC 19670261
recovered_description.gt: This piece preserves part of the right edge with the upper torso, not including the head, and the arms of the second serving woman.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment of a frieze plaque, broken on three sides. Surface is worn, with no discernible features preserved.

recovered_description.label: F1780
recovered_description.gt: A stone block in the garden of house 11. Color is white.
recovered_description.pred: Limestone block near a house. Appears weathered and partially buried. Located in a rural setting with nearby agricultural materials. Similar to other features found in residential areas of the Avkat Archaeological Project.

recovered_description.label: PC 20090228
recovered_description.gt: Squared wing handle fragment preserves a slight upward curve and is incised with a single line approximately 0.005 m away from the edge of the handle. Handle begins to increase in thickness toward its break surface, probably a vessel attachment point.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment of a vessel with a black slip on the exterior. The fragment is rectangular with a defined edge, suggesting it may be part of a rim or base. The surface shows signs of wear and pitting, consistent with other pottery fragments from the Murlo project.

recovered_description.label: PC 20120109
recovered_description.gt: Frieze plaque fragment preserves two dentils and a small raised area below, though it is impossible to tell which type of frieze plaque.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment of a frieze plaque with heavily worn and pitted surfaces, preserving no discernible decorative elements due to encrustation and visible inclusions throughout the fabric.

recovered_description.label: F1591
recovered_description.gt: Ashlar limestone, some-what smooth and is fragmented and shape as a result appears to be a broken heart. Colour is grey, yellow and white flecks. House No. 91. Diameter is 0.48m
recovered_description.pred: Pile of wooden poles and stones in a dry area. Includes a greenish metal bowl and a rusted metal object. Likely part of a farmyard or garden setting.

recovered_description.label: F1676
recovered_description.gt: One block stone in the garden wall of house 40. Color is white and gray.
recovered_description.pred: Limestone block, greyish in color, located near a dilapidated structure. Positioned along a dirt path, possibly part of a wall or foundation. Shows signs of wear and weathering, consistent with other features in the area.

recovered_description.label: PC 19680677
recovered_description.gt: Fragment of guilloche with a large part of the tile preserved (0.140 m).
recovered_description.pred: The fragment preserves a section of guilloche pattern. The lower right edge is intact, while the top and left side are broken.

recovered_description.label: 4-S-1(1665)
recovered_description.gt: Eyes (socket for pupil) for statuary. Broken on one edge.
recovered_description.pred: Elliptical eye socket from an elephant head or statue, similar to other objects found in the Petra Great Temple Excavations.

recovered_description.label: PC 19850068
recovered_description.gt: Orangeware finial is composed of two superimposed roundels.
recovered_description.pred: The vessel is entirely preserved with a circular, slightly domed head. The surface shows signs of wear and has a rough texture. The head appears to taper into a shaft, although the shaft is not visible in the image. The artifact is consistent with pottery items from the Murlo project.

recovered_description.label: 2-S-1(1082)
recovered_description.gt: Partial facial fragment with an eye on either side of the face and curling hair on the top of the head. On the lower part of the face there appear to be whiskers or feathers. The frontal area of the face has been damaged. The style is Hellenistic and could be Nabataean. The back of the head is rough and flattened, suggesting it was placed against a wall.
recovered_description.pred: Limestone fragment with rough surface, possibly part of a larger object or architectural element. No discernible features or decorations visible.

recovered_description.label: DT# 5220
recovered_description.gt: Constantius II OBV: CONSTAN-TIVS AVG pearl diadem head right REV: REV: GLOR-IA EXERC-ITVS, two soldiers holding spears and shields on either side of standard, dot on either side of standard
recovered_description.pred: unknown OBV: [-----] head or bust right REV: GLOR-IA EXERC-ITVS, two soldiers holding spears and shields on either side of standard

recovered_description.label: PC 19790086
recovered_description.gt: Frieze fragment preserves guilloche pattern and hind leg and tail of second horse. The hoof has been worn off.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment preserves a portion of the guilloche pattern along the finished edge. The surface is pitted and worn.

recovered_description.label: PC 19870136
recovered_description.gt: The fragment is roughly triangular in shape and slightly curved in section. The body is pierced by a series of small holes of irregular dimensions. Four complete ones are preserved toward the middle of the fragment while a portion of four others may be seen along the edge.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment of a fenestrated vessel with four complete circular fenestrations. The surfaces are worn, and the fragment is broken on all three sides, suggesting it was pierced from the outside.

recovered_description.label: VdM20155043
recovered_description.gt: Greek black slip fragment preserves black slip on one side and the pinkish orange fabric on the other side. The black slip completely covers one side. The side with no slip ahs two small indentations in the fabric.
recovered_description.pred: Black gloss pottery fragment with a triangular shape, preserving black gloss on one side. The opposite side is undecorated. The fragment is flat with lightly scratched surfaces and worn edges.

recovered_description.label: 94-S-1(395)
recovered_description.gt: Head fragment of yellow limestone with open mouth and most of open eye, beginning of hair
recovered_description.pred: Limestone fragment of a head, possibly an animal, with a prominent curved feature resembling a horn or ear. The surface is smooth with visible cracks. Similar to other fragments from Petra Great Temple Excavations.

recovered_description.label: 5-S-1(1753)
recovered_description.gt: Small horned altar consisted of a limestone rectangular block with two cross cuts on the upper surface, resulting in four small channels. The stone is smooth apart from a little damage on the base and a small dent on the upper surface.
recovered_description.pred: Rectangular limestone fragment with two parallel grooves on one side, possibly part of a larger architectural or decorative element.

recovered_description.label: Record 934
recovered_description.project: Differentiating local from nonlocal ceramic production at Iron Age Sardis using NAA
recovered_description.gt: Cup - gray - study sample?
recovered_description.pred: bowl - black slip, faceted

recovered_description.label: PC 19680154
recovered_description.gt: Preserves three-stranded hair from lower right of antefix. A small part of the antefix back is preserved.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment of an antefix with worn surface, broken on three sides. Possible remnants of hair or decorative elements visible, similar to other Murlo project artifacts.

recovered_description.label: PC 19680287
recovered_description.gt: Perhaps a rear corner of the back extension of a sima. The side has a raised edge which appears nearly rectangular in cross-section (the inside corner is slightly curved). This edge widens toward the back: marks made in smoothing the raised edges can be seen in the surface of the base. The back raised edge is flat on top and has an overhanging lip which projects over the base. Near the break, the lower part of this edge becomes narrower. At the break, the edge thickens and ends in an irregular surface which may be original. There is a semicircular notch (0.050 m across) in the back raised edge 0.034 m from the corner: perhaps a cover tile would have fitted into it. There are extensive traces of red paint on the base, the inside and outside surfaces of the raised edges (more remains on the back than on the side) and the bottom. The chipped areas on the outside surface of the corner also have traces of red paint.
recovered_description.pred: Fragment of a lateral sima with a chipped and grooved outer surface. The piece is worn on the bottom and features a slightly curved edge, suggesting it may have been part of a decorative architectural element.

recovered_description.label: PC 19840015
recovered_description.gt: Antler fragment shows signs of having been worked. The narrow end is cut evenly. There is a small cut mark parallel to and near the cut end. At one place the natural curve of the antler has been shaved flat.
recovered_description.pred: Rocchetto fragment with a cylindrical shaft flaring into two convex heads. The surface shows signs of wear and two incised lines on one side.

recovered_description.label: 1-B-16(927)
recovered_description.gt: Intricately carved pin crown, perforated from top to bottom, with a side hole that could be accidental.
recovered_description.pred: Bone object with triple grooves below a rounded head, similar to other artifacts with pointed or quadrilateral heads.

recovered_description.label: PC 20190022
recovered_description.gt: A molded terra cotta feline head fragment preserving a decorated finished face, undecorated unfinished face, and several finished edges. On the decorated, finished face, the edge of the top of the head curves gently into two triangular ears. The feline's left ear projects from curve 0.01928m horizontally while the feline's right ear projects vertically 0.02183m. From the top of the head the fragment increases in thickness in profile, being somewhat convex between the ears and the eyebrows. In the middle of the face are two ovoid eyes (max. pres. height: 0.01282m; max. pres. width: 0.01726m) with brow (max. pres. width: 002091m) projecting above them. The brows curve towards one another and the center of the fragment to become the nose which has three convex ridges separated by a thin indented line before the snout. The nose is max. pres. height of 0.06100m, max. pres. width of 0.02496m, and min. pres. width of 0.01648m. The narrowest nose ridge is max. pres. height of 0.00781m and max. pres. width of 0.01733m. The second largest nose ridge is max. pres. height of 0.00882m and max. pres. width of 0.01919m. The largest nose ridge is max. pres. height of 0.01236m and max. pres. width of 0.02182m. The snout is max. pres. height of 0.01742m and max. pres. width of 0.02505m. The nose is raised in profile above the cheeks which slope gently from the nose and eyes towards the side and bottom edges of the fragment. The bottom edges are somewhat curving inward concavely towards the cheeks. No clear evidence for the articulation of a mouth or jaws. On the undecorated, unfinished face at the opposite of the crown of the head is a roughly linear raised portion, max. pres. height of 0.03199m and max. pres. length of 0.09786m (the max. pres. thickness of the fragment is the thickness of the raised portion), which shows signs of having been hand-worked as well as of a possible attachment scar. The rest of the unfinished face is relatively flat, with a max. pres. thickness of 0.02713m.
recovered_description.pred: Terracotta feline head fragment, likely part of a lateral sima waterspout, preserving the central portion of the face. The surface is worn and pitted, with a modern break along one edge. The nose is prominent, and the overall shape suggests a stylized animal form, consistent with other Murlo architectural elements.

recovered_description.label: 94-P-26(206)
recovered_description.gt: Upper lamp segment with sunray rim edging and floral decoration with double volutes on nozzle and concentric ridges around small convex filling hole. Buff colored clay with black slip
recovered_description.pred: Upper segment with deeply molded ridges and central filling hole. Partial volute and sunray design present.

recovered_description.label: Sherd F-4-4023-4233-3
recovered_description.gt: triangle incised on shoulder Chalco looking ware
recovered_description.pred: incised with red paint
